# tokens: # Signature-matcher ordering spec — covers the lattice-reversed sort
# eng/go/signature.go::CompareSignatures applies to overloaded words.
# The order is:
#   1. Longer arity first (list-size dominant).
#   2. Per position, the more specific type/pattern first — reversed
#      CompareValues on the per-slot expectation value (sigSlotValue).
#   3. BarrierPos as final tiebreaker (piped sigs first).
# At each position the slot value is either the structural Pattern
# (when set) or a bare type literal of the declared arg type, so the
# unified type/value lattice settles the order — same machinery as
# `cmp` / `sort` / `lt`.
# Verification is via:
#   - `inspect k 'signatures' get` — shows the registered overloads in
#     dispatch order (post-sort).
#   - dispatching a value of the most-specific type — first-match-wins
#     reveals which sig actually fires.
# 1. User-defined scalar subtypes (refine Integer / refine String)
# A `def Pos refine Integer` mints Pos in the external scalar band
# (Rank > all kernel scalars, per typetable.go::externalBandFor), so
# Pos sorts strictly above its kernel parent in the reversed order.
def Pos refine Integer  def k fn [[Integer] [String] ['i']  [Pos] [String] ['p']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: refine Integer sorts before Integer

# tokens: def Pos refine Integer  def k fn [[Pos] [String] ['p']  [Integer] [String] ['i']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: registration order doesn't matter — the sort is stable on lattice keys

# tokens: def Phrase refine String  def k fn [[String] [String] ['s']  [Phrase] [String] ['p']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: refine String sorts before String

# tokens: # 2. User-defined node subtypes (refine List / refine Map)
# Same band rule on the Node branch — refined List / Map subtypes
# sort above their kernel parents.
def Vec refine List  def k fn [[List] [String] ['l']  [Vec] [String] ['v']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: refine List sorts before List

# tokens: def Dict refine Map  def k fn [[Map] [String] ['m']  [Dict] [String] ['d']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: refine Map sorts before Map

# tokens: # 3. User-defined Object / Record types
# `def Foo (refine Object {x:Integer})` mints Foo in the external
# Ideal band — higher Rank than the kernel Object node. Verified via
# both inspect (sort key) and dispatch (only Foo-typed instances hit
# the Foo overload).
def Foo (refine Object {x:Integer})  def k fn [[Object] [String] ['o']  [Foo] [String] ['f']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: Foo subtype sorts before Object

# tokens: def Foo (refine Object {x:Integer})  def k fn [[Object] [String] ['o']  [Foo] [String] ['f']]  make Foo {x:1} k
note: Foo instance dispatches to the Foo sig

# tokens: def Foo (refine Object {x:Integer})  def k fn [[Any] [String] ['a']  [Foo] [String] ['f']]  make Foo {x:1} k
note: specific Foo wins over the Any catch-all

# tokens: # Sibling user types: same Rank, same depth — tiebreak by name. Lex
# puts Bar < Foo in natural order; the reversed sig sort flips that to
# Foo first.
def Foo refine List  def Bar refine List  def k fn [[Foo] [String] ['f']  [Bar] [String] ['b']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: siblings tiebreak by name (reversed)

# tokens: # 4. Go-defined Ideals (Tensor / Matrix / Vector from aql:matrix)
# Matrix and Vector are registered via RegisterExternalBuiltin as
# lattice children of Tensor (modules/matrix.go::registerTensorTypes).
# They share the external Ideal band Rank with their parent; same
# Rank, deeper depth → child sorts before parent in the reversed
# order. Matrix vs Vector are siblings — name tiebreak (Matrix < Vector
# lex) flipped means Vector sorts first.
def k fn [[Tensor] [String] ['t']  [Matrix] [String] ['m']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: Matrix sorts before Tensor (child before parent)

# tokens: def k fn [[Tensor] [String] ['t']  [Matrix] [String] ['m']]  make Matrix [[1 2][3 4]] k
note: Matrix instance hits the Matrix sig

# tokens: def k fn [[Tensor] [String] ['t']  [Matrix] [String] ['m']]  make Vector [1 2 3] k
note: Vector isn't Matrix — falls through to Tensor

# tokens: def k fn [[Tensor] [String] ['t']  [Matrix] [String] ['m']  [Vector] [String] ['v']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: Matrix/Vector both before Tensor; Vector before Matrix on name tiebreak (reversed)

# tokens: def k fn [[Tensor] [String] ['t']  [Matrix] [String] ['m']  [Vector] [String] ['v']]  make Vector [1 2 3] k
note: Vector instance hits the Vector sig

# tokens: # User refinement OF a Go-defined Ideal: deeper than Matrix, same
# external band → sorts ahead of Matrix.
def UMat refine Matrix  def k fn [[Matrix] [String] ['m']  [UMat] [String] ['u']]  inspect k 'signatures' get
note: user-defined subtype of Matrix sorts before Matrix

# tokens: # 5. Pattern-driven sort (concrete Pattern beats bare type literal)
# A sig with a concrete-value Pattern sorts strictly ahead of a sig
# with the bare type — via litVsConcreteOrder on the family Comparer
# (CompareValues path), reversed by the sig sorter so the more
# specific overload runs first.
def k fn [[0] [String] ['z']  [Integer] [String] ['n']]  k 0
note: value 0 matches the 0-pattern

# tokens: def k fn [[0] [String] ['z']  [Integer] [String] ['n']]  k 5
note: value 5 falls to the bare Integer overload

# tokens: def k fn [['x'] [String] ['xx']  [String] [String] ['s']]  k 'x'
note: String pattern beats bare String

# tokens: def k fn [['x'] [String] ['xx']  [String] [String] ['s']]  k 'y'
note: non-matching String falls to bare

# tokens: # Map patterns: OpenUnifyMap subset-matches, so a larger pattern is
# strictly more specific than a smaller one. CompareValues orders
# maps by size first → reversed → larger map pattern sorts first.
def k fn [[{a:1}] [String] ['a']  [{a:1 b:2}] [String] ['ab']]  k {a:1 b:2}
note: the larger-pattern sig wins

# tokens: def k fn [[{a:1}] [String] ['a']  [{a:1 b:2}] [String] ['ab']]  k {a:1}
note: smaller value matches only the smaller pattern
